Perplexity: sonar (⚠ web-augmented) — INFORMATIONAL ONLY. All Perplexity API models use web search; no offline model exists. Primary fair comparison: IDSS vs GPT-catalog-bound vs Sajjad.

- **Pros (Rare Value)**: Covers accidents/liquid damage post-warranty; useful if mobile-heavy (e.g., hospital rotations)[2][5].
- **Cons (Majority View)**: Overpriced vs. self-repair/replacement; better to save the $60–$150 for a future upgrade[5][7].

**Recommendation**: Skip it unless you're accident-prone. Use manufacturer support first, then budget for repairs (~$100–$300 common fixes)[1][2]. For your Vivobook/Aspire/IdeaPad picks, check retailer deals for bundled options under $100 if desired[3][4].
